title: Force chainsaw autoplay at 60% volume, remove sound controls, and add clown-laugh autoplay fallback

requirements.text: Update the background audio behavior so the chainsaw loop is forced on by default: set the chainsaw loop volume to 60% (0.6 on the HTMLAudioElement volume scale), attempt to autoplay on page load, and remove the user-facing sound on/off control and any "Enable sound" prompt UI from the site.

quotes: Add the sound should be 60 loud, audio should automatically plays, and if he doesn’t work add the new fallback is a clown laughing

acceptanceCriteria: The chainsaw audio element uses the existing file path "/assets/audio/chainsaw-loop.mp3" and loops continuously.
The chainsaw audio volume is set to 0.6.
On page load, the app attempts to start playback automatically without requiring the user to click a sound button.
No sound on/off toggle button is rendered anywhere (including the fixed bottom-right icon button).
No "Enable Sound" prompt UI is shown (including the fixed top-right prompt card).

requirements.text: Add an automatic clown-laugh fallback audio that the site attempts to play when the chainsaw autoplay attempt is blocked or fails; the clown-laugh fallback should loop and should be attempted automatically (no user-facing controls).

quotes: add clown laugh if website tries to block it prevent website from blocking it

acceptanceCriteria: A new static audio asset exists at "/assets/audio/clown-laugh.mp3" (or equivalent under frontend/public/assets/audio so it is reachable at that URL).
If the initial chainsaw autoplay attempt fails/throws, the app attempts to autoplay the clown-laugh audio automatically as a fallback.
The clown-laugh fallback loops continuously when it is the active audio.
The fallback logic does not introduce new UI controls/buttons for audio.

requirements.text: Implement best-effort mitigation for browser autoplay restrictions without adding audio UI controls: after a blocked autoplay attempt, re-attempt starting the chainsaw loop (and then the clown-laugh fallback if needed) on common first-user-gesture events (e.g., click/tap, keydown, or pointerdown anywhere on the page) while keeping the experience control-free.
acceptanceCriteria: When autoplay is blocked on initial load, the app registers first-user-gesture listeners and automatically re-attempts playback once a gesture occurs (without showing a dedicated audio prompt UI).
After playback successfully starts once, the gesture listeners are cleaned up to avoid repeated calls.
No additional audio-related UI is added as part of this mitigation.

constraints: Do not modify any files under frontend/src/components/ui or other paths listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Backend changes are not required; keep all audio logic on the frontend and serve audio as static assets.
nonGoals: Do not add third-party audio libraries or streaming services.
Do not implement backend audio serving or persistence for audio settings.
Do not add additional ambient tracks beyond the existing chainsaw loop and the new clown-laugh fallback.
